headline: FLOOR ENTITY CONDUCT DOCUMENTATION — NO SCORE CHANGE. Composite remains 0 / Critical / Floor. The May 7 2026 Winmana village hospital airstrike in Kani Township, Sagaing Region (two PDF members receiving medical treatment killed) is documented in this window as continuation and entrenchment of a systematic medical-facility targeting pattern, not a new structural-change designation. The May 6 (Chin State Tedim Township, 9 killed including 5 women in PDF base) and May 2 (Kanpetlet bridge repair, 9 civilians killed including children) events bracket the May 7 hospital attack, establishing a near-daily pattern of civilian and medical-facility targeting consistent with the April 2026 UN Special Rapporteur final report (127x airstrike escalation 2021-2025) already documented at floor record. Per floor-entity protocol, composite cannot move below floor; new in-window evidence is appended to the floor-exit-criteria record. Cross-reference: Amnesty International December 2025 documentation of 'deadly military air strike on hospital shows vicious disregard for right to life' establishes hospital targeting as a documented pattern category (not novel); the May 7 Winmana incident is a continuation. Recommendation: confirm floor; append systematic medical-facility targeting evidence to the floor record; surface the pattern in the next digest.
floor_protocol_note: Floor-designated entity (Myanmar designated prior). Per floor-protocol, composite cannot move below floor; in-window evidence is appended to the floor-exit-criteria record only. No dimensional movement proposed. The May 7 Winmana hospital airstrike entrenches the systematic medical-facility-targeting pattern already documented (Amnesty December 2025, UN SR April 2026). Sustained pattern, not a new structural-change designation.
key_evidence: May 7 2026 (in-window): Junta airstrike on hospital in Winmana village, Kani Township, Sagaing Region; two PDF members receiving medical treatment killed. Hospital targeting documented while patients under care — direct violation of medical-facility protections under international humanitarian law (Geneva Conventions Art. 18 Fourth Convention; AP I Art. 12).
May 6 2026 (in-window): Junta airstrike Chin State Tedim Township; nine killed including five women in PDF base.
May 2 2026 (in-window): Junta airstrike Kanpetlet Township, Chin State; nine civilians killed at bridge repair operation, including children.

Pattern category established: Amnesty International December 2025 documented prior 'deadly military air strike on hospital shows vicious disregard for right to life' — May 7 Winmana is continuation of an Amnesty-documented pattern category, not a novel conduct type.

Cross-reference: UN Independent Investigative Mechanism for Myanmar (IIMM) has called for information on multiple deadly hospital airstrikes including Rakhine State precedent — IIMM evidence-collection infrastructure active in window.

UN Special Rapporteur Tom Andrews April 2026 final report to HRC (already appended to floor record May 5 cycle): airstrikes 9 (2021) → 1,140 (2025), 127x escalation; 3.6M+ displaced; 12M+ acute hunger; ~33% of population requires humanitarian assistance. The May 2-7 cluster is consistent with the documented pattern magnitude.

Floor exit criteria not in trajectory: cessation of attacks + restoration of humanitarian access + leadership accountability mechanism. Continued medical-facility targeting moves the entity further from floor-exit criteria, not toward them.

evidence.source: Moemaka — Daily casualties amid ongoing airstrikes
evidence.finding: Daily casualty documentation including May 7 Sagaing hospital airstrike (Winmana village, Kani Township), May 6 Tedim Township, May 2 Kanpetlet — establishes near-daily airstrike pattern in window.

evidence.source: Mizzima English — Kanpetlet bridge repair strike
evidence.finding: May 2 Kanpetlet civilian strike; nine killed including children at bridge repair operation.

evidence.source: Fortify Rights — Investigation: destruction of schools, churches, medical facilities (November 2025)
evidence.finding: Documented systematic targeting of schools, churches, and medical facilities — establishes November 2025 baseline for the pattern category continuing in May 2026 window.

evidence.source: Amnesty International — Myanmar: Deadly military air strike on hospital shows vicious disregard for right to life (December 2025)
evidence.finding: December 2025 hospital airstrike documented as 'vicious disregard for right to life' — establishes Amnesty pattern-category designation for hospital airstrikes. May 7 Winmana incident is continuation.

evidence.source: OHCHR / UN Special Rapporteur April 2026 (carried from prior cycle)
evidence.finding: 127x airstrike escalation 2021-2025; UN call for international action on impunity — the May 2-7 cluster is consistent with the documented escalation magnitude.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 1 unchanged (floor). Continued medical-facility-targeting pattern entrenches suffering-detection absence at perpetrator level.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 1 unchanged (floor). Airstrikes are direct harm production; the May 7 hospital incident targets patients receiving medical care — anti-response action.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 1 unchanged (floor). Aid disproportionately blocked in opposition areas (Sagaing, Chin State are PDF-active regions targeted).
scoring_rationale.BND: 1 unchanged (floor). Hospital targeting is a categorical IHL boundary failure (medical-facility protections under Geneva Conventions).
scoring_rationale.ACC: 1 unchanged (floor). No domestic accountability; UN SR explicitly calling for international action because domestic mechanisms absent. IIMM evidence collection continuing.

new_conduct_categories_notated: May 7 2026 hospital airstrike (Winmana village, Kani Township, Sagaing) targeting PDF patients receiving medical treatment — continuation of Amnesty-documented hospital-targeting pattern category, NOT new structural designation
May 2-7 2026 near-daily airstrike cluster across Sagaing and Chin State entrenching systematic civilian and medical-facility targeting pattern
Hospital-targeting pattern category formally appended to floor-exit-criteria record: continuation of attacks on medical facilities moves the entity further from floor-exit criteria
structural_change_finding: NO structural change. The May 7 Winmana hospital airstrike is a continuation of the Amnesty December 2025 / Fortify Rights November 2025 documented hospital-targeting pattern, not a new conduct category. Per floor-entity protocol the existing floor designation and dimensional scores are sustained; the in-window evidence is appended to the floor record and surfaced in the digest for pattern visibility.
negativeSignals: May 7 hospital airstrike (Winmana, Sagaing) targeting patients under medical care; May 6 Tedim airstrike (9 killed including 5 women); May 2 Kanpetlet bridge airstrike (9 civilians including children); systematic medical-facility targeting pattern entrenched; 127x airstrike escalation 2021-2025; bad-faith ceasefire pattern sustained; IIMM evidence collection ongoing.
sector_context: Floor-cluster countries. Myanmar pattern of medical-facility targeting is part of a documented systematic pattern (Amnesty, Fortify Rights, UN SR, IIMM); the May 7 Winmana incident is continuation, not novel. Cross-reference Sudan (active genocide pattern), South Sudan (UNMISS regression), Israel (extraterritorial interdiction, 72,599 Palestinian deaths per OCHA April 29). The floor cluster as of 2026-05-08 includes Sudan, South Sudan, Myanmar, Israel (countries), Palantir AI, xAI/Grok, Character.AI (ai-labs), Ghost Robotics (robotics-labs) — 8 entities, no change this cycle.

Watch any IIMM formal preservation finding on May 7 hospital airstrike
Floor-exit criteria sustain: cessation of attacks + restoration of humanitarian access + leadership accountability mechanism — none in trajectory
Continued daily monitoring for any structural-change-trigger event (e.g., extension of targeting pattern beyond Sagaing and Chin)
